title: Validating Git Tag Names with Long Names

statement.markdown: Write a function that validates whether a given string is a valid Git tag name. A valid Git tag name should not be too long (less than 40 characters), nor should it be too short (less than 1 character), and it should not be empty. Additionally, it should not contain any special characters except for hyphens and underscores. The function should return true if the string is a valid Git tag name and false otherwise.
statement.notes: The function should not consider tag names with leading or trailing spaces as valid.

hints.text: Use regular expressions to validate the string.

editorial.title: Validating Git Tag Names
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to use regular expressions to validate the string.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would be to check each character of the string manually, but this would be inefficient and prone to errors.
editorial.betterSolution: Using regular expressions can simplify the validation process and make it more efficient.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a regular expression to match the pattern of a valid Git tag name.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The regular expression used will match any string that is less than 40 characters, contains only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores, and is not empty.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to consider the length of the string.
